title: Java Garbage Collection Process
description: A flowchart illustrating the lifecycle of a Java object and the process by which it becomes eligible for, and is eventually reclaimed by, the garbage collector.
elements: Object No Longer Referenced (Eligible for GC)
relationships: An object is created and remains in use while referenced. When references are removed, it becomes eligible for GC. The JVM's Garbage Collector eventually runs, may call finalize(), and finally reclaims the memory.
